rawText:
The biggest lesson that can be read from 70 years of AI research is that general methods that leverage computation are ultimately the most effective, and by a large margin. The ultimate reason for this is Moore's law, or rather its generalization of continued exponentially falling cost per unit of computation. Most AI research has been conducted as if the computation available to the agent were constant (in which case leveraging human knowledge would be one of the only ways to improve performance) but, over a slightly longer time than a typical research project, massively more computation inevitably becomes available. Seeking an improvement that makes a difference in the shorter term, researchers seek to leverage their human knowledge of the domain, but the only thing that matters in the long run is the leveraging of computation. These two need not run counter to each other, but in practice they tend to. Time spent on one is time not spent on the other. There are psychological commitments to investment in one approach or the other. And the human-knowledge approach tends to complicate methods in ways that make them less suited to taking advantage of general methods leveraging computation. There were many examples of AI researchers' belated learning of this bitter lesson, and it is instructive to review some of the most prominent.

In computer chess, the methods that defeated the world champion, Kasparov, in 1997, were based on massive, deep search. At the time, this was looked upon with dismay by the majority of computer-chess researchers who had pursued methods that leveraged human understanding of the special structure of chess. When a simpler, search-based approach with special hardware and software proved vastly more effective, these human-knowledge-based chess researchers were not good losers. They said that "brute force" search may have won this time, but it was not a general strategy, and anyway it was not how people played chess. These researchers wanted methods based on human input to win and were disappointed when they did not.

A similar pattern of research progress was seen in computer Go, only delayed by a further 20 years. Enormous initial efforts went into avoiding search by taking advantage of human knowledge, or of the special features of the game, but all those efforts proved irrelevant, or worse, once search was applied effectively at scale. Also important was the use of learning by self play to learn a value function (as it was in many other games and even in chess, although learning did not play a big role in the 1997 program that first beat a world champion). Learning by self play, and learning in general, is like search in that it enables massive computation to be brought to bear. Search and learning are the two most important classes of techniques for utilizing massive amounts of computation in AI research. In computer Go, as in computer chess, researchers' initial effort was directed towards utilizing human understanding (so that less search was needed) and only much later was much greater success had by embracing search and learning.

sentences.original: The biggest lesson that can be read from 70 years of AI research is that general methods that leverage computation are ultimately the most effective, and by a large margin.
sentences.translation: AI 연구 70년에서 읽어낼 수 있는 가장 큰 교훈은, 연산을 활용하는 범용적 방법이 궁극적으로 가장 효과적이며, 그것도 큰 차이로 그렇다는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: that can be read from 70 years of AI research
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (that — lesson을 수식)

grammarElements.text: that general methods that leverage computation are ultimately the most effective
grammarElements.label: that절 (보어, 내부에 관계대명사절 포함)

vocabulary.example: Companies leverage technology to gain competitive advantage.
The problem requires enormous computation.
Ultimately, the simpler approach proved better.
She won the election by a wide margin.

expressions.usage: 우위의 정도가 매우 클 때 사용하는 표현
데이터나 경험에서 교훈을 도출할 때

patterns.pattern: The biggest lesson [from X] is that [main claim]
patterns.explanation: 논문이나 에세이에서 핵심 주장을 문장 첫머리에 제시하는 학술적 오프닝 패턴.
examples: The biggest takeaway from the study is that early intervention matters most.
The main lesson from history is that empires eventually fall.

drillQuestions.question: The biggest lesson that _____ be read from 70 years of AI research...
General methods are the most effective, and by a large _____.

sentences.notes: 에세이의 핵심 테제(thesis)를 첫 문장에서 제시한다. that이 세 번 등장: (1) "lesson that" (관계대명사), (2) "is that" (보어절 접속사), (3) "methods that" (관계대명사). 이 중첩 구조가 학술적 글쓰기의 특징이다.

koreanAlignment.en: that can be read from 70 years of AI research
is that general methods that leverage computation

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 논제 선언(thesis statement) — 에세이 전체의 핵심 주장을 첫 문장에 압축하여 제시

sentences.original: The ultimate reason for this is Moore's law, or rather its generalization of continued exponentially falling cost per unit of computation.
sentences.translation: 이에 대한 궁극적인 이유는 무어의 법칙, 더 정확히 말하면 연산 단위당 비용이 기하급수적으로 지속적으로 하락하는 그 일반화이다.

grammarElements.text: its generalization of continued exponentially falling cost per unit of computation
grammarElements.label: 보어 (정정된 표현)

vocabulary.example: The data is growing exponentially.
This principle is a generalization of Newton's law.
The cost per unit has decreased over time.

expressions.usage: 이전 표현을 더 정확하게 바로잡을 때
기술적 비용 효율성을 논할 때

patterns.pattern: The reason for this is [X], or rather [more precise X]
patterns.explanation: 원인을 제시한 뒤 "or rather"로 더 정확한 표현으로 수정하는 학술적 패턴.
examples: The cause is inflation, or rather the specific monetary policies that drive it.
The answer is experience, or rather the systematic accumulation of deliberate practice.

drillQuestions.question: The ultimate reason for this is Moore's law, or _____ its generalization.
The cost per _____ of computation keeps falling.

sentences.notes: "or rather"는 자기 수정(self-correction) 표현으로, 처음 한 말을 더 정확하게 고치는 역할. Moore의 법칙(트랜지스터 수가 2년마다 두 배)을 더 넓은 원리(연산 비용의 기하급수적 하락)로 일반화하고 있다.

sentences.original: Most AI research has been conducted as if the computation available to the agent were constant (in which case leveraging human knowledge would be one of the only ways to improve performance) but, over a slightly longer time than a typical research project, massively more computation inevitably becomes available.
sentences.translation: 대부분의 AI 연구는 에이전트가 사용할 수 있는 연산이 일정하다는 전제 하에 수행되었고(그 경우에는 인간의 지식을 활용하는 것이 성능을 향상시키는 거의 유일한 방법일 것이다), 그러나 전형적인 연구 프로젝트보다 약간 더 긴 시간에 걸쳐 훨씬 더 많은 연산이 필연적으로 사용 가능해진다.

grammarElements.text: as if the computation available to the agent were constant
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (가정법 — as if + were)

grammarElements.text: in which case leveraging human knowledge would be one of the only ways to improve performance
grammarElements.label: 삽입 관계절 (in which case)

grammarElements.text: over a slightly longer time than a typical research project
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (시간)

grammarElements.text: massively more computation inevitably becomes available
grammarElements.label: 주절 (대조)

vocabulary.example: The experiment was conducted over three years.
The AI agent learns from its environment.
Technology inevitably changes how we work.
Computing power has massively increased.

expressions.usage: 실제와 다른 가정을 나타내는 가정법 구문. 학술 글에서 잘못된 전제를 지적할 때 사용
앞선 가정의 결과를 설명할 때
선택지가 극히 제한적임을 강조

patterns.pattern: [Research] has been conducted as if [assumption], but [reality]
patterns.explanation: 연구의 잘못된 전제를 가정법으로 제시한 뒤, but으로 현실을 대조하는 학술적 비판 패턴.
examples: Education has been designed as if all students learned the same way, but in reality learning styles vary greatly.
Economic policy has been conducted as if growth were unlimited, but resources are finite.

drillQuestions.question: AI research has been conducted as if computation _____ constant.
Massively more computation _____ becomes available.

sentences.notes: 이 에세이에서 가장 긴 문장 중 하나. 가정법 과거(were constant)는 "실제로는 일정하지 않은데 마치 일정한 것처럼"이라는 비판을 담고 있다. 괄호 안의 삽입절은 가정법 귀결절(would be)로, 가정이 맞다면의 결과를 설명한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Most AI research has been conducted
as if the computation available to the agent were constant
koreanAlignment.ko: 에이전트가 사용할 수 있는 연산이 일정하다는 듯이

koreanAlignment.en: in which case leveraging human knowledge would be one of the only ways
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 경우 인간 지식을 활용하는 것이 거의 유일한 방법일 것이다

koreanAlignment.en: but, over a slightly longer time
koreanAlignment.ko: 그러나 약간 더 긴 시간에 걸쳐

koreanAlignment.en: massively more computation inevitably becomes available
koreanAlignment.ko: 훨씬 더 많은 연산이 필연적으로 사용 가능해진다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 전제 비판(premise critique) — 주류 연구의 암묵적 가정을 가정법으로 드러내어 비판

sentences.original: Seeking an improvement that makes a difference in the shorter term, researchers seek to leverage their human knowledge of the domain, but the only thing that matters in the long run is the leveraging of computation.
sentences.translation: 단기적으로 차이를 만드는 개선을 추구하면서, 연구자들은 해당 분야에 대한 인간의 지식을 활용하려 하지만, 장기적으로 중요한 유일한 것은 연산의 활용이다.

grammarElements.text: Seeking an improvement that makes a difference in the shorter term
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (현재분사 — 동시 동작/이유)

grammarElements.text: their human knowledge of the domain
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: but the only thing that matters in the long run
grammarElements.label: 대조절 주어 (관계대명사 포함)

vocabulary.example: Scientists seek to understand the universe.
She is an expert in the domain of natural language processing.
In the long run, practice beats talent.

expressions.usage: 의미 있는 변화를 가져올 때
장기(in the long run)와 대비하여 단기를 강조할 때
핵심 요소를 다른 모든 것에서 분리하여 강조

patterns.pattern: Seeking [short-term goal], [subject] [verb], but the only thing that matters in the long run is [X]
patterns.explanation: 분사구문으로 단기적 동기를 제시한 뒤, but으로 장기적으로 진정 중요한 것을 대비시키는 패턴.
examples: Seeking quick profits, investors chase trends, but the only thing that matters in the long run is fundamentals.
Seeking immediate comfort, people avoid challenges, but the only thing that matters in the long run is growth.

drillQuestions.question: _____ an improvement in the shorter term, researchers use human knowledge.
The only thing that matters in the long _____ is computation.

sentences.notes: "Seeking"으로 시작하는 분사구문이 연구자들의 동기를 설명하고, 주절에서 같은 동사 "seek"이 반복되어 문체적 연결을 만든다. "in the shorter term"과 "in the long run"의 시간적 대비가 에세이의 핵심 논지를 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Seeking an improvement that makes a difference in the shorter term
their human knowledge of the domain
but the only thing that matters in the long run

sentences.original: These two need not run counter to each other, but in practice they tend to.
sentences.translation: 이 둘이 반드시 서로 반대 방향으로 갈 필요는 없지만, 실제로는 그런 경향이 있다.

grammarElements.label: 동사 (need not — 부정, run counter to — 상충하다)
주절 (tend to — 뒤에 run counter 생략)

vocabulary.example: His actions run counter to his words.
In theory it works, but in practice it fails.
People tend to overestimate their abilities.

expressions.usage: 영국식 영어에서 불필요성을 표현. "don't need to"보다 격식적
두 접근법이나 아이디어가 서로 반대로 작용할 때

patterns.pattern: [A] and [B] need not [conflict], but in practice they tend to.
patterns.explanation: 이론적 양립 가능성을 인정한 뒤, 현실에서의 충돌을 지적하는 양보-대조 패턴.
examples: Work and life need not conflict, but in practice they tend to.
Speed and quality need not be opposed, but in practice they tend to be.

drillQuestions.question: These two need not run _____ to each other.
In practice, they _____ to conflict.

sentences.notes: "need not"는 조동사 need의 부정으로, "don't need to"보다 격식적이다. "they tend to" 뒤에 "run counter to each other"가 생략되어 있다(동사구 생략). 짧은 문장이지만 논리적 깊이가 있다.

koreanAlignment.en: These two need not run counter to each other
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 둘이 반드시 서로 상충할 필요는 없다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 양보-대조(concession-contrast) — 이론적 가능성을 인정한 뒤 현실적 제약을 제시

sentences.original: Time spent on one is time not spent on the other.
sentences.translation: 하나에 쓰는 시간은 다른 하나에 쓰지 않는 시간이다.

grammarElements.text: time not spent on the other
grammarElements.label: 보어 (과거분사 부정형)

vocabulary.example: Time spent learning is never wasted.

expressions.phrase: time spent on X is time not spent on Y
expressions.meaning: X에 쓰는 시간은 Y에 쓰지 않는 시간이다
expressions.usage: 자원(특히 시간)의 기회비용을 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: [Resource] spent on [A] is [resource] not spent on [B]
patterns.explanation: 기회비용(opportunity cost)을 표현하는 패턴. 자원의 제로섬 특성을 간결하게 보여준다.
examples: Energy spent on worrying is energy not spent on solving.
Money invested in A is money not available for B.

drillQuestions.question: Time _____ on one is time not spent on the other.

sentences.notes: 10단어의 짧은 문장이지만 경제학의 기회비용(opportunity cost) 개념을 완벽하게 압축한다. 대칭 구조(time spent on X / time not spent on Y)가 기억에 남는 격언 같은 효과를 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: is time not spent on the other

pronunciationNotes: spent: /spent/ — "스펜트", 자연스럽게 끝의 t는 약하게 발음
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대구법(parallelism) + 격언(aphorism) — 대칭적 구조로 기회비용이라는 개념을 간결하게 압축

sentences.original: There are psychological commitments to investment in one approach or the other.
sentences.translation: 하나의 접근법 또는 다른 접근법에 대한 투자에 심리적 몰입이 존재한다.

grammarElements.text: to investment in one approach or the other
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (대상)

vocabulary.example: There is a psychological barrier to changing habits.
Success requires a strong commitment to practice.

expressions.usage: 한 방향에 이미 투자한 것을 포기하지 못하는 심리적 경향
두 가지 중 하나를 선택하는 상황을 표현

patterns.pattern: There are [psychological/emotional] commitments to [X]
patterns.explanation: 특정 접근법에 대한 심리적 집착이나 매몰 비용 효과를 설명하는 패턴.
examples: There are emotional commitments to traditional methods.
There are institutional commitments to existing infrastructure.

drillQuestions.question: There are psychological _____ to investment in one approach.
Investment in one approach _____ the other.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 매몰 비용 오류(sunk cost fallacy)와 확증 편향(confirmation bias)이라는 심리학 개념을 암시한다. 연구자들이 한 접근법에 시간과 경력을 투자하면 그것을 포기하기 어려워진다는 인간적 한계를 지적.
pronunciationNotes: psychological: /ˌsaɪkəˈlɑːdʒɪkl/ — "사이컬라지컬", p는 묵음, 강세는 세 번째 음절

sentences.original: And the human-knowledge approach tends to complicate methods in ways that make them less suited to taking advantage of general methods leveraging computation.
sentences.translation: 그리고 인간 지식 접근법은 방법론을 복잡하게 만드는 경향이 있어, 연산을 활용하는 범용적 방법의 이점을 취하기에 부적합하게 만든다.

grammarElements.text: in ways that make them less suited to taking advantage of general methods leveraging computation
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (관계대명사절 포함, 방식)

vocabulary.example: Adding too many features can complicate the system.
This tool is well suited to the task.
We should take advantage of this opportunity.

expressions.phrase: in ways that make them less suited to
expressions.usage: 부정적 결과를 간접적으로 표현하는 학술적 표현
하이픈으로 복합 수식어를 만들어 하나의 개념으로 통합

patterns.pattern: [Approach A] tends to complicate [X] in ways that make [X] less suited to [Y]
patterns.explanation: 한 접근법이 다른 접근법의 이점을 간접적으로 방해하는 메커니즘을 설명하는 패턴.
examples: Micromanagement tends to complicate workflows in ways that make them less suited to innovation.
Regulation tends to complicate markets in ways that make them less suited to rapid adaptation.

drillQuestions.question: The approach tends to _____ methods in harmful ways.
Methods become less _____ to taking advantage of computation.

sentences.notes: 하이픈(-) 복합어 "human-knowledge approach"와 "search-based approach" 등은 학술적 글쓰기의 특징. "in ways that"은 방식을 설명하는 관계사절로, 학술 영어에서 인과관계를 간접적으로 표현할 때 자주 사용된다.

koreanAlignment.en: in ways that make them less suited to

sentences.original: There were many examples of AI researchers' belated learning of this bitter lesson, and it is instructive to review some of the most prominent.
sentences.translation: AI 연구자들이 이 쓰라린 교훈을 뒤늦게 깨달은 사례가 많이 있었으며, 가장 두드러진 사례들을 검토하는 것은 교훈적이다.

grammarElements.text: many examples of AI researchers' belated learning of this bitter lesson
grammarElements.label: 실질 주어 (소유격 + 명사구)

grammarElements.text: to review some of the most prominent
grammarElements.label: 진주어 (to부정사)

vocabulary.example: He sent a belated birthday card.
The failure was instructive for future projects.
She is a prominent figure in the field.
It was a bitter defeat for the team.

expressions.usage: 마땅히 알았어야 할 것을 늦게 배운 상황
사례 분석의 가치를 설명하며 논의를 전개할 때

patterns.pattern: There were many examples of [X], and it is instructive to review [some]
patterns.explanation: 사례의 풍부함을 언급한 뒤, 학술적 검토의 필요성을 제시하는 전환 패턴.
examples: There were many cases of corporate failure, and it is instructive to examine the patterns.
There are numerous examples of this phenomenon, and it is worth examining a few.

drillQuestions.question: AI researchers' _____ learning of this lesson is well documented.
It is _____ to review some of the most prominent examples.

sentences.notes: 에세이 제목 "The Bitter Lesson"이 본문에 직접 등장하는 문장. "researchers' belated learning"은 소유격 + 명사 구조로, 연구자들이 뒤늦게 배웠다는 의미. 이 문장은 도입부에서 구체적 사례로 전환하는 역할을 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: and it is instructive to review

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 전환(transition) — 이론적 주장에서 역사적 사례 분석으로의 서론적 전환

sentences.original: In computer chess, the methods that defeated the world champion, Kasparov, in 1997, were based on massive, deep search.
sentences.translation: 컴퓨터 체스에서, 1997년 세계 챔피언 카스파로프를 꺾은 방법은 대규모의 깊은 탐색에 기반했다.

grammarElements.text: the methods that defeated the world champion, Kasparov, in 1997
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계대명사절 + 동격어구 포함)

vocabulary.example: The algorithm defeated all human competitors.
The project required massive computational resources.
The decision was based on the available data.

expressions.usage: AI/게임 이론에서 가능한 수를 깊이 탐색하는 방법

patterns.pattern: In [field], the methods that [achieved X] were based on [approach]
patterns.explanation: 특정 분야에서 성공한 방법론을 소개하는 학술적 사례 제시 패턴.
examples: In machine translation, the methods that achieved state-of-the-art results were based on neural networks.
In drug discovery, the methods that succeeded were based on computational screening.

drillQuestions.question: The methods that defeated Kasparov were _____ on massive search.
In computer chess, _____ search proved most effective.

sentences.notes: Kasparov는 1997년 IBM의 Deep Blue에 패배했다. 이 사건은 AI 역사의 이정표. 동격어구(appositive) "Kasparov"가 쉼표로 "the world champion"을 구체화한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the methods that defeated the world champion, Kasparov
were based on massive, deep search

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 역사적 사례(historical exemplum) — 구체적 사건(Deep Blue vs Kasparov)으로 추상적 논지를 뒷받침

sentences.original: At the time, this was looked upon with dismay by the majority of computer-chess researchers who had pursued methods that leveraged human understanding of the special structure of chess.
sentences.translation: 당시, 이것은 체스의 특수한 구조에 대한 인간의 이해를 활용하는 방법을 추구해 온 대다수의 컴퓨터 체스 연구자들에 의해 실망스럽게 받아들여졌다.

grammarElements.text: by the majority of computer-chess researchers
grammarElements.label: 행위자 (by + 명사구)

grammarElements.text: who had pursued methods that leveraged human understanding of the special structure of chess
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (who — 내부에 또 다른 관계대명사절 that 포함)

vocabulary.example: The news was met with dismay.
She pursued a career in medicine.
The majority of students passed the exam.

expressions.usage: 어떤 결과나 사태를 부정적으로 인식할 때
분야 고유의 특성을 언급할 때

patterns.pattern: [This result] was looked upon with [emotion] by [group] who had pursued [alternative]
patterns.explanation: 특정 결과에 대한 기존 연구자 집단의 감정적 반응을 묘사하는 패턴.
examples: The discovery was looked upon with skepticism by scientists who had pursued different theories.
The policy change was looked upon with frustration by advocates who had pursued gradual reform.

drillQuestions.question: This was looked upon with _____ by the researchers.
Researchers had _____ methods that leveraged human understanding.

sentences.notes: 관계대명사가 이중으로 중첩: who(연구자들) → that(방법). 과거완료(had pursued)는 Deep Blue의 승리보다 더 이전부터 연구해 왔음을 나타낸다. "human understanding of the special structure"는 인간 지식 접근법의 구체적 내용.

koreanAlignment.en: this was looked upon with dismay
by the majority of computer-chess researchers
of the special structure of chess

sentences.original: When a simpler, search-based approach with special hardware and software proved vastly more effective, these human-knowledge-based chess researchers were not good losers.
sentences.translation: 특수 하드웨어와 소프트웨어를 갖춘 더 단순한 탐색 기반 접근법이 훨씬 더 효과적임이 증명되었을 때, 이 인간 지식 기반 체스 연구자들은 좋은 패자가 아니었다.

grammarElements.text: When a simpler, search-based approach with special hardware and software
grammarElements.label: 종속절 주어 (when + 복합 수식)

vocabulary.example: The method proved effective in testing.
The new system is vastly superior.
He is not a good loser and often makes excuses.

expressions.usage: 패배 후 변명하거나 결과를 폄하하는 태도를 비판할 때
AI에서 가능한 해법을 체계적으로 탐색하는 방법론

patterns.pattern: When [simple method] proved [vastly/far] more effective, [proponents of complex method] were not good losers
patterns.explanation: 단순한 방법이 승리했을 때 기존 연구자들의 비합리적 반응을 묘사하는 서사 패턴.
examples: When data-driven methods proved more accurate, traditional analysts were not good losers.
When automation proved more efficient, the displaced workers were understandably resentful.

drillQuestions.question: The search-based approach _____ vastly more effective.
These researchers were not good _____.

sentences.notes: "not good losers"는 일상적인 관용 표현("패배를 깔끔하게 인정하지 않는 사람")을 학술 에세이에 사용한 것으로, Sutton의 문체적 특징인 직설적이고 비격식적인 어조를 보여준다. 하이픈 복합어가 3개나 등장한다.

koreanAlignment.ko: 이 인간 지식 기반 체스 연구자들은

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 아이러니(irony) — 학술 에세이에 일상적 관용구를 사용하여 연구자들의 태도를 신랄하게 비판

sentences.original: They said that "brute force" search may have won this time, but it was not a general strategy, and anyway it was not how people played chess.
sentences.translation: 그들은 "무차별 대입" 탐색이 이번에는 이겼을 수 있지만, 그것은 범용적 전략이 아니며, 어쨌든 사람들이 체스를 두는 방식이 아니라고 말했다.

grammarElements.text: "brute force" search may have won this time
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (may have + 과거분사)

grammarElements.text: but it was not a general strategy
grammarElements.label: 대조절

grammarElements.text: and anyway it was not how people played chess
grammarElements.label: 추가 대조절 (anyway — 어쨌든)

vocabulary.example: The hacker used brute force to crack the password.
We need a general strategy that works across different situations.
The plan failed, but anyway we learned from it.

expressions.usage: 사실을 인정하면서도 그 의미를 축소하려 할 때
인간의 방식과 다르다는 것을 비판으로 사용

patterns.pattern: [X] may have [succeeded] this time, but it was not [general], and anyway it was not how [humans did it]
patterns.explanation: 성과를 인정하면서도 세 가지 이유로 그 의미를 축소하는 변명의 패턴.
examples: AI may have beaten the test this time, but it was not understanding, and anyway it was not how humans think.

drillQuestions.question: "Brute force" search may _____ won this time.
It was not how people _____ chess.

sentences.notes: "brute force"에 따옴표가 쳐져 있는 것은 패배한 연구자들이 경멸적으로 사용한 표현임을 나타낸다. 세 가지 변명(이번만 이겼다 / 범용적이지 않다 / 인간의 방식이 아니다)은 점점 더 감정적으로 변한다. Sutton은 이를 통해 인간 편향을 꼬집고 있다.

koreanAlignment.en: "brute force" search may have won this time
koreanAlignment.ko: "무차별 대입" 탐색이 이번에는 이겼을 수 있지만

koreanAlignment.en: but it was not a general strategy
it was not how people played chess

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 간접 인용을 통한 반박(reported speech as critique) — 패배자들의 변명을 그대로 옮김으로써 그 비합리성을 드러냄

sentences.original: These researchers wanted methods based on human input to win and were disappointed when they did not.
sentences.translation: 이 연구자들은 인간의 입력에 기반한 방법이 이기기를 원했고, 그렇지 않았을 때 실망했다.

vocabulary.example: The system requires minimal human input.
The team was disappointed by the results.

expressions.usage: want + 목적어 + to부정사 구조
반복을 피하기 위해 앞선 동사(win)를 생략

patterns.pattern: [Subject] wanted [X] to [succeed] and were disappointed when [X] did not
patterns.explanation: 기대와 실망을 간결하게 대비시키는 서사적 패턴.
examples: Investors wanted the startup to succeed and were disappointed when it did not.
Fans wanted the team to win and were devastated when they did not.

drillQuestions.question: They wanted methods based on human input _____ win.
They were disappointed when they did _____.

sentences.notes: "when they did not"에서 win이 생략되어 있다(동사 대용). 이 문장은 연구자들의 편향을 직접적으로 지적한다: 과학적 결과보다 자신이 선호하는 방법의 승리를 원했다. Sutton의 핵심 비판 — 연구자의 감정적 편향이 과학적 진보를 방해한다.

koreanAlignment.en: methods based on human input to win

sentences.original: A similar pattern of research progress was seen in computer Go, only delayed by a further 20 years.
sentences.translation: 비슷한 연구 진보의 패턴이 컴퓨터 바둑에서도 나타났는데, 다만 20년 더 늦게였다.

grammarElements.text: A similar pattern of research progress
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: only delayed by a further 20 years
grammarElements.label: 과거분사구 (부가 정보)

vocabulary.example: The same pattern repeats across many fields.
No further action is needed.
The project was delayed by several months.

expressions.usage: 유사한 현상이 시간 차이를 두고 반복될 때

expressions.phrase: a similar pattern was seen in
expressions.usage: 다른 분야에서의 유사 사례를 도입할 때

patterns.pattern: A similar pattern [of X] was seen in [field], only [modifier]
patterns.explanation: 앞선 사례의 패턴이 다른 분야에서도 반복됨을 연결하는 학술적 전환 패턴.
examples: A similar pattern of adoption was seen in mobile technology, only accelerated.
A similar trend was observed in Europe, only with a 10-year lag.

drillQuestions.question: A _____ pattern of research progress was seen in computer Go.
It was delayed by a _____ 20 years.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 체스 단락에서 바둑 단락으로의 전환 문장. "only delayed by a further 20 years"는 분사구문으로, 독립된 절이 아니라 주절에 부가적 정보를 제공. AlphaGo가 2016년에 이세돌을 이겼으므로 1997년 Deep Blue로부터 약 20년 뒤.

koreanAlignment.en: A similar pattern of research progress
only delayed by a further 20 years

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 반복 구조(recurring motif) — 체스와 바둑의 평행적 역사를 통해 논지의 보편성을 강화

sentences.original: Enormous initial efforts went into avoiding search by taking advantage of human knowledge, or of the special features of the game, but all those efforts proved irrelevant, or worse, once search was applied effectively at scale.
sentences.translation: 인간의 지식이나 게임의 특수한 특징을 활용하여 탐색을 회피하려는 막대한 초기 노력이 투입되었지만, 탐색이 대규모로 효과적으로 적용되자 그 모든 노력은 무의미한 것으로, 아니 더 나쁜 것으로 드러났다.

grammarElements.text: avoiding search by taking advantage of human knowledge, or of the special features of the game
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (동명사구)

grammarElements.text: but all those efforts proved irrelevant, or worse
grammarElements.label: 대조절 (prove + 형용사)

grammarElements.text: once search was applied effectively at scale
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (시간 — once)

vocabulary.example: The project required enormous resources.
Experience became irrelevant in the new system.
The technology works well at scale.
Years of work went into the research.

expressions.usage: 부정적 평가를 한 단계 더 격상시킬 때 삽입하는 표현
결정적 전환점을 나타내는 once 절

patterns.pattern: Enormous efforts went into [avoiding X], but all those efforts proved [negative], once [Y] was applied at scale
patterns.explanation: 대규모 노력이 수포로 돌아간 상황을 서술하는 패턴. 반전(twist)을 만들어 논지를 강화.
examples: Enormous efforts went into manual curation, but all proved irrelevant once automated systems were deployed at scale.

drillQuestions.question: Enormous efforts went _____ avoiding search.
All those efforts proved _____ once search was applied at scale.

sentences.notes: "or worse"라는 삽입어는 강한 수사적 효과. 단순히 "무의미"한 정도가 아니라 오히려 "해로웠다"는 함의. "at scale"은 실리콘밸리/기술 업계에서 흔히 사용되는 표현으로, 대규모 적용을 의미한다.

koreanAlignment.en: by taking advantage of human knowledge
or of the special features of the game
but all those efforts proved irrelevant, or worse
koreanAlignment.ko: 하지만 그 모든 노력은 무의미한, 아니 더 나쁜 것으로 드러났다

koreanAlignment.en: once search was applied effectively at scale

sentences.original: Also important was the use of learning by self play to learn a value function (as it was in many other games and even in chess, although learning did not play a big role in the 1997 program that first beat a world champion).
sentences.translation: 자기 대전(self play)을 통한 학습으로 가치 함수를 학습하는 것도 중요했다(다른 많은 게임에서, 심지어 체스에서도 그러했지만, 학습은 세계 챔피언을 처음 이긴 1997년 프로그램에서는 큰 역할을 하지 않았다).

grammarElements.text: the use of learning by self play to learn a value function
grammarElements.label: 주어 (도치됨)

grammarElements.text: as it was in many other games and even in chess
grammarElements.label: 비교절 (as — ~처럼)

grammarElements.text: although learning did not play a big role in the 1997 program that first beat a world champion
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (although)

vocabulary.meaning: 자기 대전 (AI가 자기 자신과 대결하며 학습)
vocabulary.example: AlphaGo improved through millions of games of self play.

vocabulary.meaning: 가치 함수 (게임 상태의 가치를 평가하는 함수)
vocabulary.example: The agent learns a value function to evaluate board positions.

expressions.usage: 추가적 중요 사항을 도치로 강조하여 제시할 때

expressions.phrase: did not play a big role in
expressions.usage: 기여도가 낮았음을 표현

patterns.pattern: Also important was [X] (as it was in [Y], although [exception])
patterns.explanation: 도치 구문으로 추가 정보를 강조하고, 괄호 안에 비교와 예외를 포함하는 학술적 패턴.
examples: Also important was the role of data quality, as it was in previous studies, although the effect was smaller.

drillQuestions.question: Also important _____ the use of learning by self play.
Learning did not _____ a big role in the 1997 program.

sentences.notes: "Also important was X"는 보어가 문두로 나온 도치(inversion) 구문. 일반 어순은 "The use of learning was also important." 도치는 학술 영어에서 정보의 흐름을 조절하는 데 사용된다. 괄호 안의 긴 삽입은 맥락을 제공하지만 주장의 흐름을 방해하지 않도록 처리.

koreanAlignment.en: the use of learning by self play
as it was in many other games and even in chess
koreanAlignment.ko: 다른 많은 게임에서, 심지어 체스에서도 그러했듯이

koreanAlignment.en: although learning did not play a big role
koreanAlignment.ko: 비록 학습이 큰 역할을 하지는 않았지만

koreanAlignment.en: in the 1997 program that first beat a world champion

sentences.original: Learning by self play, and learning in general, is like search in that it enables massive computation to be brought to bear.
sentences.translation: 자기 대전에 의한 학습, 그리고 일반적인 학습은, 대규모 연산을 투입할 수 있게 한다는 점에서 탐색과 같다.

grammarElements.text: Learning by self play, and learning in general
grammarElements.label: 주어 (동명사구 + 삽입적 확장)

grammarElements.text: it enables massive computation to be brought to bear
grammarElements.label: 이유/설명절 (in that절)

vocabulary.example: All available resources were brought to bear on the problem.
Machine learning in general has made great progress.
Technology enables faster communication.

expressions.usage: 유사성이나 이유의 구체적 근거를 제시할 때
대규모 자원을 특정 문제에 집중적으로 적용할 때

patterns.pattern: [X] is like [Y] in that it enables [Z]
patterns.explanation: 두 개념의 유사성을 "in that"으로 구체적으로 설명하는 비교 패턴.
examples: Reading is like travel in that it broadens your perspective.
Coding is like writing in that it requires iterative refinement.

drillQuestions.question: Learning is like search _____ that it enables massive computation.
Massive computation can be brought to _____.

sentences.notes: "brought to bear"는 격식적인 관용구로, 군사 용어에서 유래. "대포를 적에게 겨누다"에서 "자원을 문제에 집중시키다"로 확장. "in that"은 "because"와 유사하지만 더 학술적이고 정밀한 인과관계를 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: massive computation to be brought to bear

sentences.original: Search and learning are the two most important classes of techniques for utilizing massive amounts of computation in AI research.
sentences.translation: 탐색과 학습은 AI 연구에서 대규모 연산을 활용하기 위한 가장 중요한 두 부류의 기법이다.

grammarElements.text: the two most important classes of techniques
grammarElements.label: 보어 (서수 + 최상급)

grammarElements.text: for utilizing massive amounts of computation
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (목적 — for + 동명사)

vocabulary.example: There are two classes of algorithms for this problem.
Machine learning techniques have advanced rapidly.
Utilizing data effectively is crucial for AI.

expressions.phrase: the two most important classes of
expressions.usage: 핵심 범주를 정확히 두 가지로 요약할 때
기술적 규모를 표현

patterns.pattern: [A] and [B] are the two most important [categories] for [purpose]
patterns.explanation: 핵심 범주 두 가지를 선언적으로 정리하는 학술적 요약 패턴.
examples: Supply and demand are the two most important concepts for understanding markets.
Theory and practice are the two most important pillars for effective education.

drillQuestions.question: Search and learning are the two most important _____ of techniques.
These techniques are for _____ massive amounts of computation.

sentences.notes: 에세이의 핵심 분류: AI에서 연산을 활용하는 방법은 크게 "탐색(search)"과 "학습(learning)" 두 가지. 이 간결한 분류가 에세이 전체의 분석 프레임워크를 구성한다.

koreanAlignment.en: are the two most important classes of techniques
for utilizing massive amounts of computation

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 핵심 분류(key taxonomy) — 복잡한 논의를 두 가지 핵심 개념으로 정리하여 독자의 이해를 돕는 선언적 요약

sentences.original: In computer Go, as in computer chess, researchers' initial effort was directed towards utilizing human understanding (so that less search was needed) and only much later was much greater success had by embracing search and learning.
sentences.translation: 컴퓨터 바둑에서, 컴퓨터 체스에서와 마찬가지로, 연구자들의 초기 노력은 인간의 이해를 활용하는 방향으로(탐색이 덜 필요하도록) 향했고, 훨씬 나중에야 탐색과 학습을 수용함으로써 훨씬 더 큰 성공이 달성되었다.

grammarElements.text: In computer Go, as in computer chess
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 비교구 (as in — ~에서와 마찬가지로)

grammarElements.text: so that less search was needed
grammarElements.label: 목적절 (so that)

grammarElements.text: and only much later was much greater success had by embracing search and learning
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (도치 — only much later + 동사 도치)

vocabulary.example: Efforts were directed towards improving efficiency.
Embracing new technology is essential for progress.
The initial results were promising.

expressions.usage: 이전 사례와의 유사성을 간결하게 연결
only + 시간 표현이 문두에 오면 주어-동사 도치가 발생

expressions.phrase: so that less search was needed
expressions.usage: 목적이나 의도를 설명하는 so that 절

patterns.pattern: In [field B], as in [field A], [initial approach], and only much later was [better result] had by [correct approach]
patterns.explanation: 두 분야를 병렬로 비교하며, 도치를 사용해 "뒤늦은 올바른 접근"을 강조하는 패턴.
examples: In NLP, as in vision, early work focused on rules, and only much later was progress made by embracing data-driven methods.

drillQuestions.question: Researchers' initial effort was directed _____ utilizing human understanding.
Only much later _____ much greater success had.

sentences.notes: "only much later was ... had"는 부정/제한 부사(only)가 문두에 오면서 발생하는 도치(inversion). 일반 어순은 "much greater success was had only much later." "success was had"는 격식적인 수동태로, 일상 영어에서는 드물지만 학술 영어에서 사용된다. 이 문장은 바둑 단락의 결론으로, 체스와의 평행 구조를 완성한다.

koreanAlignment.en: researchers' initial effort was directed towards
so that less search was needed
and only much later was much greater success had
koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 훨씬 나중에야 훨씬 더 큰 성공이 달성되었다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 도치(inversion) + 평행 구조(parallel structure) — "only much later"를 문두에 배치하여 뒤늦은 깨달음을 강조하고, 체스 사례와의 구조적 반복으로 논지를 강화
